rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al VS. GCS, INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, Requests For Admissions And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 17, 2011, Line 1. PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, Requests For Admissions And Request For Production Of Documents. Hearing required unless all issues raised by the motion have been resolved. The court received copies of the statements filed by the parties and once again encourages the parties to engage in still further meet and confer to resolve or narrow any remaining issues and report on their progress in doing so at the hearing. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 16, 2011, Line 1, PLAINTIFFs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: M DEE SAMUELS VS. RALPH TASHJIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 16, 2011, Line 5, PLAINTIFF M SAMUELS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Plaintiff satisfied her burden of production and defendant did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue as to either liability or amount owed. Plaintiff¿s objections to the declarations of Mr. Bigger and Mr. Tashjian are sustained. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and adding language that complies with CCP 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALDYTH BELTANE et al VS. FRANK HARIRI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Party Affiliated Witness Ernesto Santos Campos/Quality Construction Corp. To Respond To And Produce Documents And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 17, 2011, Line 4. PLAINTIFFS ALDYTH BELTANE, BRAD BELTANE, TRICIA D'AVANZO, MICHAEL DAWSON, JOHN MOUNTAIN, RODNELL SEGAL, ALICIA VOSBERG, HEATHER FRANCO AND JOANN WU'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Party Affiliated Witness Ernesto Santos Campos/Quality Construction Corp. To Respond To And Produce Documents And Request For Sanctions. Denied without prejudice to be re-filed solely against the subpoenaed parties. Sanctions denied. The motion was made on the incorrect premise that defendants are responsible for the discovery deficiencies of the subpoenaed parties on the grounds that the subpoenaed parties are ¿party-affiliated¿ witnesses. They are not. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA BY AND et al VS. ACADEMIE DE CUISINE LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 16, 2011, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT CHINESE NEWCOMERS SERVICE CENTER'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled with ten days to answer. The court cannot determine at this stage of the case that the cross-defendant is neither a joint tortfeasor nor a co-obligor nor that the only liability that can be imposed on cross-complainant is for intentional wrongdoing for which indemnity is barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA BY AND et al VS. ACADEMIE DE CUISINE LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 16, 2011, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT CHINESE NEWCOMERS SERVICE CENTER'S AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. Denied. In ruling on the motion, the court must assume that the factual allegations of the cross-complaint are true. The cross-complaint arises out of the same transaction, occurrence, or series of transactions or occurrences as the claims alleged in the complaint and thus is permissible under CCP 428.10(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE GLANTZ VS. BAC HOME LOANS SERVICING, LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 16, 2011, Line 20, DEFENDANT QUALITY LOAN SERVICE CORPORATION DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to all causes of action that are the subject of the demurrer. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if she can do so in good faith, to allege that she had an enforceable contract with Quality Loan Service, Quality Loan Service made a promise to her which it should be estopped from denying, and Quality Loan Service invaded her privacy and acted with malice to defeat the qualified privilege of Civil Code 2924(b). New allegations are to be in boldface type. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARK CLEMENS VS. U-HAUL COMPANY OF CALIFORNIA, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Arbitration, To Stay Action Pending Arbitration And Dismiss Paga Allegations; Table Of Non-Cal Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 16, 2011, Line 21, DEFENDANT U-HAUL COMPANY OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Compel Arbitration, To Stay Action Pending Arbitration And Dismiss Paga Allegations; Table Of Non-Cal Authorities. Granted. Defendant established that the electronic signature is attributable to plaintiff per CC 1633.9(a) and thus the arbitration agreement is valid. The PAGA waiver is unenforceable per Brown v. Ralph¿s Grocery Company (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 489, 502-03. However, the PAGA waiver is severable from the rest of the agreement because the PAGA waiver is the only portion of the agreement that is unconscionable and severing the PAGA waiver would not require reformation of the entire agreement. The PAGA waiver is therefore severed from the rest of the agreement. Plaintiff is required to arbitrate all of his claims except the PAGA claims. The PAGA claims are tayed pending the outcome of the arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
